growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron lifted its U.S. investment to more than $250 billion through 2035 and signed a 10-year wafer supply deal with GlobalWafers, locking in the HBM and DRAM capacity AI workloads are short of.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI HBM demand, domestic supply-chain investment, and a low forward multiple make Micron the cleanest risk/reward in the batch.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Apple extended its custom-chip deal through 2031 and said Broadcom will help produce more than 15 billion U.S.-made chips, reinforcing Broadcom's edge-AI and connectivity monetization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom combines elite FCF, AI custom-silicon growth, VMware software leverage, and a de-risked Apple relationship.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Axios and Breaking Defense said the U.K.'s new Defence Investment Plan sets aside about GBP 298 billion over four years, including GBP 2.5 billion for cyber and electronic warfare, GBP 5 billion for autonomous systems, and GBP 790 million for homeland air defenses, all categories that map well to L3Harris's communications, sensing, and EW franchises.
growth_picks.analyst_take: L3Harris offers defense-electronics exposure with record backlog, recent contract wins, and more valuation support than RTX.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft's orchestration push in Copilot and Foundry gives it a platform to route enterprise AI workloads across models while it trims lower-margin units to fund the buildout.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft combines durable cloud and AI platform growth with a near-term earnings catalyst and a valuation that is reasonable after the drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: UHS is a profitable healthcare operator trading more than a third below its high while still generating solid earnings and cash flow, which makes the drawdown look more like sentiment than broken economics.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Universal Health Services offers rare cheap quality with near-term earnings, strong Q1 results, and more than 30% target upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TTD is sitting at $19.48 versus a 52-week high of $91.45 after a brutal 2026 reset, but it still screens as profitable with a $9.18B market cap and the next earnings call is due on August 6, 2026; that gives a real catalyst window after the market has already priced in a lot of bad ad-tech news. Bear case: programmatic ad budgets and partner churn could keep the stock cheap if management does not show a clean re-acceleration.
growth_picks.analyst_take: A 78% drawdown, high short interest, positive FCF, and August earnings create an asymmetric contrarian setup if ad-tech growth stabilizes.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Ross Bhappu bought 74,000 shares for about $967,920 on July 7 and director Bruce Hansen bought 4,000 shares for about $50,780 on July 8, giving Energy Fuels a clear two-insider cluster buy in a uranium name with macro tailwinds.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels has a rare insider cluster buy plus uranium and rare-earth optionality, but the negative FCF profile keeps this speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: A signed $19 billion, 20-year Anthropic lease for 401 MW at the Justified Data campus gives TeraWulf long-dated contracted AI revenue and validates its pivot from bitcoin mining to power-secured compute campuses.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The Anthropic lease materially upgrades TeraWulf from bitcoin-mining optionality to contracted AI infrastructure, but execution risk is high.

growth_picks.source_detail: From scanner universe
growth_picks.analyst_take: Alphabet's Cloud and Search AI acceleration support buying weakness, but AI capex, antitrust, and talent-loss concerns cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RMD is 30.6% below its 52-week high of $293.81 even after years of solid execution, but it remains profitable with EPS of $10.37, a $29.57B market cap, and a confirmed fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings call on August 6, 2026 that also includes full-year FY27 outlook. Bear case: the stock is still not statistically cheap, so reimbursement, demand, or margin disappointment could limit the rebound.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ResMed is a quality medical-device compounder trading 30% below its high while GLP-1 fears look over-discounted.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Fiserv has been reset hard after its 2025 guidance shock, but it still screens as a profitable fintech with a low multiple and recent buying interest around a depressed share price.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fiserv is a messy turnaround, but a 70% drawdown, very low multiple, insider buying, and strategic-asset optionality justify a contrarian buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Reuters coverage republished by Investing.com on June 22 said China added MP Materials to its export-control list in retaliation for U.S. restrictions, reinforcing a multi-year rare-earth squeeze that keeps MP's domestic mine-to-oxide chain strategically valuable.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is a strategic rare-earth asset with policy support and supply-chain scarcity, but negative FCF and insider selling keep it speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: EIA's July 8 update showed U.S. energy exports remain at record levels, while market coverage on July 10 said renewed U.S.-Iran conflict is reviving the global gas risk premium, which should favor Cheniere's expanding LNG export network and long-duration contracting model.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere has durable LNG contract exposure, raised guidance, and geopolitical tailwinds, though leverage and limited drawdown reduce upside quality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Globalharvest Holdings Venture Ltd, a 10% owner, kept adding to Mission Produce on July 6-8 with three buys of 650,415 shares at $12.73, 491,865 shares at $13.40, and 687,222 shares at $13.42, or roughly $24.1 million total.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Mission Produce has one of the strongest insider-buying signals in the batch and a plausible Calavo-driven turnaround setup.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Barchart flagged a March 19, 2027 ratio backspread in QXO where a trader sold 12,000 $18 calls for about $2.868 million and bought 24,000 $25 calls for about $2.4 million, taking in a $468,000 credit while betting on a big move.
growth_picks.analyst_take: QXO is an aggressive Brad Jacobs roll-up with a fresh TopBuild close, large revenue base, and unusual bullish options activity, but integration risk is substantial.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Rezolve AI combines a 1.04 billion market cap, 15.79% short float and 2.39 days to cover on Finviz with Barchart's note of a $300 million buyback and accelerating revenue, which makes it a credible squeeze setup if execution holds.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Speculative AI-commerce squeeze setup with a large approved buyback and very high reported revenue acceleration, but operating losses and liquidity risk keep conviction low.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: LULU is down 50.7% from its 52-week high of $241.84 but still only about 13% above the low, and it remains very profitable with EPS of $12.38 and a 9.63x P/E; the next earnings date is estimated for September 3, 2026, which gives a clear catalyst while the market continues to punish the brand. Bear case: North America demand and product missteps may take longer to fix than the market is willing to wait for.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lululemon is cheap for its margin profile after a deep drawdown, but the buy case depends on stabilizing Americas demand and avoiding new China missteps.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: DocuSign is still deeply discounted, but recent commentary argues the software selloff may have overshot while the stock remains fundamentally profitable and still generating cash.
growth_picks.analyst_take: DocuSign's cash generation and low forward multiple outweigh modest growth concerns as IAM adoption and AI workflow partnerships improve the story.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: BofA argues that cheaper AI-native design tools and uncertain AI monetization threaten Adobe's growth profile even after its recent earnings beat and growing AI recurring revenue.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is priced like an ex-growth software name despite elite margins, huge FCF, and early AI ARR traction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: KeyBanc's downgrade says Agentforce still lacks traction and agentic AI could pressure the SaaS model more than the current valuation reflects.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce is a contrarian buy on valuation and FCF after the Agentforce selloff, but proof of AI adoption is now the central catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Guardian reporting on July 7 said NATO allies are being pushed toward 5% of GDP defense spending while Europe worries the U.S. may not be able to keep supplying Patriot and HIMARS interceptors, which directly supports RTX's missile-defense backlog and air-defense mix.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has excellent defense demand and a July earnings catalyst, but valuation and limited target upside argue against chasing near highs.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Brookfield expanded its Bloom partnership to $25 billion, tying Bloom's solid-oxide fuel cells to the AI data-center power bottleneck and backing a 2026 revenue guide of $3.4 billion to $3.8 billion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Bloom is a real AI power beneficiary, but the valuation, leverage, and massive share-price run leave insufficient entry discipline.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: A linked 10% owner and director bought 610,291 shares for about $7.2M, which is a large, high-conviction insider signal in a newly public cybersecurity name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Netskope has cybersecurity growth and insider-buy signals, but valuation, losses, and post-IPO trading noise make it too early to underwrite.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Viatris has a clear binary regulatory event on the calendar, with the FDA assigning a July 30, 2026 PDUFA date for its low-dose estrogen weekly patch.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Viatris is cheap and cash-generative with a near-term FDA catalyst, but leverage and limited upside make it a hold rather than a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Eaton is directly exposed to the industrial power and data-center capex theme that has been attracting flow into infrastructure-oriented names.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Eaton is a high-quality data-center power winner, but the current price already discounts much of the electrical backlog story.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Quanta sits in the middle of the grid and data-center buildout trade, and recent flow commentary has highlighted infrastructure as one of the market's strongest rotation buckets.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Quanta has one of the best grid and data-center backlogs in industrials, but the valuation is too aggressive for a new buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: OilPrice said on July 10 that refining margins for gasoline and diesel jumped to record highs after Middle East tensions re-escalated and Russia tightened diesel exports, a direct crack-spread tailwind for Valero's Gulf Coast refining system.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Valero is benefiting from strong crack spreads, but the stock is near its high and analyst upside is essentially exhausted.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Dollar Tree announced a new $2.5 billion share repurchase authorization on July 2 and said it had already repurchased $500 million in June, leaving about $700 million under the prior authorization, a sizeable capital-return signal at a depressed valuation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Capital return is improving after the new buyback authorization, but valuation and analyst upside are not compelling enough to chase.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Meta's Iris chip plan and fresh calls for wafer-fab spending to ramp sharply keep Applied Materials in the center of the AI equipment cycle as hyperscalers pull more capex into fabrication tools.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Applied Materials is a high-quality AI wafer-fab equipment beneficiary, but the stock already prices in much of the cycle strength.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CMG has been sold down to $35.25, about 37% below its 52-week high of $55.75, while still producing positive EPS of $1.09 and carrying a $45.22B market cap; Q2 2026 earnings are scheduled for July 29, 2026, so there is a near-term reset point if the market is too pessimistic on comps and margins. Bear case: the multiple is still rich, and any soft same-store-sales read-through could keep de-rating pressure in place.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Chipotle has a real July earnings reset catalyst, but slowing comps and a still-premium multiple make the risk/reward only fair.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: SOFI has been chopped from $32.73 to $18.78, leaving it 42.6% below the high while still above the recent low and producing positive EPS of $0.44 with a $24.09B market cap; the next earnings release is confirmed for July 29, 2026, and the 14.94% short float gives it squeeze potential if growth and credit stay intact. Bear case: valuation is still demanding and any credit deterioration would hit sentiment hard.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SoFi's growth and short-interest setup are attractive, but credit and valuation risk argue for waiting for the July earnings print.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NKE has been cut almost in half from its 52-week high of $80.17 to $43.42, yet it still has a $65.71B market cap, positive EPS of $2.10, and a confirmed next earnings date of September 29, 2026; the stock is washed out enough that any sign of inventory and margin stabilization could matter. Bear case: the turnaround is slow, and another weak read on sales or margins would validate the bear case instead of reversing it.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nike has credible insider support and early turnaround signs, but current fundamentals do not yet prove a durable revenue inflection.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The U.K. plan's GBP 220 million Project NYX and wider shift toward cheap autonomous strike systems point to more demand for small armed drones and loitering munitions, giving AeroVironment a direct European procurement tailwind even before a named contract is announced.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AeroVironment has excellent drone and defense tailwinds, but valuation, negative FCF, and contract execution risk keep it below buy status.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Aqua Capital, Ltd., a 10% owner, bought 40,000 shares at $20.81 on July 8 and another 40,000 shares at $20.25 on July 7, a roughly $1.6 million accumulation that reads like institutional confidence.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider buying and a low P/E are not enough to offset Energizer's extreme leverage and shrinking revenue.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Meta's reported push to monetize its own excess AI cloud capacity hit CoreWeave, but analysts still argue GPU compute demand remains tight enough to support a long runway for leased AI infrastructure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CoreWeave has enormous AI infrastructure demand, but the balance sheet and cash burn make the equity too fragile after the IPO hype.
